audit_metadata.scope: src/ directory, database migrations, environment configuration

risk_assessment.recommendation: Address all critical and high-severity vulnerabilities before production deployment

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded Supabase Credentials in .env File
vulnerabilities.description: Supabase anon key and URL are hardcoded in .env file. If .env is checked into version control or exposed, attackers can access the entire database using the public anon key.
vulnerabilities.impact: Complete database compromise. Attackers can read all transactions, balances, and user preferences without authentication.
remediation: Move credentials to CI/CD secrets only (never commit .env)
Add .env to .gitignore immediately
Rotate Supabase anon key in production
Use separate keys for dev/staging/production
Consider using Supabase Service Role for sensitive operations with additional RLS policies

vulnerabilities.title: WebAuthn Challenge Not Validated Server-Side
vulnerabilities.description: Biometric registration and verification generate random challenges but don't validate them server-side. An attacker could replay authentication tokens or bypass WebAuthn protections.
vulnerabilities.impact: WebAuthn bypass: Attackers can forge authentication claims without legitimate biometric verification.
remediation: Store challenge server-side (in Supabase) before sending to client
Verify returned challenge signature matches stored challenge
Add challenge expiration (60 second timeout recommended)
Implement nonce-based replay protection
Add CSRF tokens to biometric registration endpoints

vulnerabilities.title: Backup Data Stored Unencrypted
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
vulnerabilities.description: Backup data containing full transaction history, balances, and user preferences is stored as plaintext JSON in the database without encryption. Vulnerable to database breach.
vulnerabilities.impact: If database is breached, all historical financial data is exposed. User financial records can be stolen without additional decryption effort.
remediation: Encrypt backup_data before storage using AES-256-GCM
Store encryption key in Supabase Vault or external key management service
Use client-side encryption before sending to server
Add authenticated encryption to prevent tampering
Implement key rotation policy (yearly minimum)

vulnerabilities.title: Authentication Tokens Stored in localStorage
vulnerabilities.description: Supabase stores JWT tokens in localStorage. This is vulnerable to XSS attacks where malicious JavaScript can steal auth tokens.
vulnerabilities.impact: XSS attack can steal authentication tokens, allowing attacker to impersonate user and access all their financial data.
remediation: Use httpOnly cookies for token storage instead of localStorage
Set Secure flag on cookies (HTTPS only)
Set SameSite=Strict to prevent CSRF
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) to prevent XSS
Add token rotation on every request

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Input Validation on Transaction Amounts
vulnerabilities.description: Transaction amounts are converted to numbers without validation. No checks for negative values, excessively large values, or non-numeric input.
vulnerabilities.impact: Attackers can create invalid transactions with extreme values, causing data corruption or financial miscalculations.
remediation: Validate amount is positive number
Set maximum transaction limit (e.g., $999,999.99)
Validate precision (max 2 decimal places)
Use schema validation library (zod, yup)
Add database constraints for amount field

vulnerabilities.title: Null Safety Issue in getBiometricSettings
vulnerabilities.description: getBiometricSettings returns null when biometric_settings is null, but callers may not handle null properly, leading to runtime errors or security bypasses.
vulnerabilities.impact: Calling code that doesn't check for null could enable biometric-protected features unintentionally.
remediation: Add null checks at all call sites
Consider throwing error instead of returning null
Add TypeScript strict null checking
Implement default settings fallback
Add runtime validation of returned object shape

vulnerabilities.title: Auth State Change Listener Not Unsubscribed
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-401: Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
vulnerabilities.description: The onAuthStateChange listener is registered at module level but never unsubscribed. If app supports component remounting, this could accumulate listeners and cause memory leaks.
vulnerabilities.impact: Memory leak over time. Multiple event listeners could pile up, causing performance degradation.
remediation: Store unsubscribe function and call on cleanup
Use useEffect cleanup pattern in React
Add listener counter to monitor active listeners
Implement listener deduplication

vulnerabilities.title: Error Messages May Leak Sensitive Information
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message
vulnerabilities.description: console.error logs detailed error messages including Supabase errors, which could expose internal structure or sensitive data.
vulnerabilities.affected_code:
console.error('Backup creation failed:', error);
console.error('Restore failed:', error);
vulnerabilities.impact: Developers/attackers with console access can see detailed error info, revealing database structure and internal operations.
remediation: Log errors to secure backend logging only
Show generic error messages to users
Sanitize error messages before logging
Implement error tracking service (Sentry, Rollbar)
Remove console.error from production builds

vulnerabilities.title: Missing HTTPS Enforcement in WebAuthn RP
vulnerabilities.description: Biometric RP ID is set to window.location.hostname without verifying HTTPS. While browsers block WebAuthn on HTTP, this is poor practice.
vulnerabilities.impact: In edge cases, could enable WebAuthn attacks if accessed over HTTP or in non-standard environments.
remediation: Explicitly set RP ID to production domain
Add HTTPS enforcement check
Document that HTTPS is required
Add fallback error if non-HTTPS detected

vulnerabilities.title: No Rate Limiting on Authentication Attempts
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-307: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames
vulnerabilities.description: No rate limiting on login attempts. Attackers can perform brute force attacks to guess credentials or biometric challenges.
vulnerabilities.impact: Brute force attacks possible for password/biometric verification.
remediation: Implement rate limiting on auth endpoints (5 attempts per 15 min)
Use Supabase Auth's built-in rate limiting
Add exponential backoff on failures
Monitor and alert on suspicious auth patterns

data_protection: No encryption at rest for financial data
No field-level encryption for sensitive data
No audit logging of data access
testing: 0% security test coverage (4,749 TODO markers in tests)
No security test cases for auth flows

immediate_actions.description: Immediately rotate anon key and regenerate new one in Supabase console. Exposed JWT tokens could be valid for years.
Run 'git filter-branch' to remove .env from entire git history. Add to .gitignore. Regenerate all exposed credentials.
Add AES-256-GCM encryption for backup_data column. Store keys in Supabase Vault.

immediate_actions.action: Migrate auth tokens to httpOnly cookies
immediate_actions.description: Update Supabase client config to use cookies instead of localStorage. Set Secure + SameSite=Strict.
Store challenges in database, validate on assertion. Implement 60-second expiration.

short_term: Implement input validation schema for all form inputs
Create security test suite (Phase 1: critical tests)
long_term: Achieve 80%+ test coverage with security-focused tests
Implement end-to-end encryption for sensitive data
Add threat modeling and security review process

compliance.gdpr: NOT COMPLIANT - No encryption of financial data, no audit logging, no data anonymization
compliance.pci_dss: NOT APPLICABLE - No credit card data stored, but similar security standards should apply to financial data

phase_1_critical: Auth listener memory leak test (30 min)

summary.status: PRODUCTION NOT READY - Multiple critical security gaps must be addressed before deployment
